passage: The restaurant on Elm Street has been a neighborhood favorite for over thirty years, known for its brick-oven pizza and homemade pasta. The owner, Marco DiNapoli, greets nearly every customer by name. He [1]_____ the restaurant with his wife in 1993 after emigrating from Naples, Italy.

passageMetadata.source: Adapted from a local business profile

stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
explanation: This question tests tense selection with a specific past time marker. 'In 1993' establishes a definite past time, requiring simple past 'opened.' The subject 'He' is singular third person.

distractorRationale.B: Correct. Simple past matching the 1993 time marker.
distractorRationale.C: Present perfect—used for indefinite past, but 'in 1993' is definite.
distractorRationale.D: Base form—wrong tense and doesn't agree with singular 'He.'
